name: Barm Brack (Traditional Irish Bread)
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Mixed dry fruit, currants, dark & golden raisins.
recipeInstructions:
1. Place dried fruit in a bowl and cover with the hot tea, allowing it to soak overnight.
2. The next day, add the remaining ingredients and mix well.
3. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
4. Pour batter into a greased 7-inch square pan and bake in the center of the oven for 1 1/2 hours.
5. Let cool in the pan on a wire rack. Slice and serve with buttered tea.
Note: Mixed spices consist of equal parts of cinnamon, clove, nutmeg, allspice, and mace.
1. In Northern Ireland and in the Republic, BRACK is the Celtic word for salt and is used to mean "bread". Barm brack is leavened bread; the word BARM meaning yeast.
2. The term "barmbrack" for an Irish fruit loaf or cake does not derive from barm or leaven. It is a corruption of the Irish word "aran breac" (Speckled Bread).
